definition: An astrononmical body part which delimited by physical discontinuities with its surroundings.
label: geographic feature

elements.http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#com: This class is being depopulated and will be filled only with inferred subclasses. Please do not use this for direct annotation, favouring instead a more descriptive subclass.

definition.value: A cavity developed along a bedding-plane and elongate in cross-section as a result.

definition.value: An anthropogenic geographic feature is a geographic feature resulting from the influence of human beings on nature.

http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_00001.value: An anthropogenic geographic feature is a geographic feature resulting from the influence of human beings on nature.

definition.value: Mine tailings are the materials left over after the process of separating the valuable fraction from the uneconomic fraction (gangue) of an ore.

definition.value: Artificial watercourse with no flow or a controlled flow used for navigation, drainage or irrigation.

definition: This class refers to the water contained in a canal channel, and not the channel itself.

definition: A marine water body which is constitutes the majority of an astronomical body's hydrosphere.
label: ocean

definition.value: A large expanse of saline water usually connected with an ocean.

definition.value: A geographical feature associated with water with a halinity above 30 ppt (roughly 35 g/L).

definition.value: A lake whose water contains a considerable concentration of dissolved salts.

definition.value: A body of water or other liquid of considerable size contained in a depression on a landmass.

definition.value: A lake of whose water contains low concentrations of salts.

definition.value: A stream which, through permanent or seasonal flow processes, moves from elevated land towards lower elevations through a definite channel and empties either into a sea, lake, or another river or ends on land as bed seepage and evapotranspiration exceed water supply.

"There are no official definitions for the generic term river as applied to geographic features, although in some countries or communities a stream is defined by its size. Many names for small rivers are specific to geographic location; examples are "run" in some parts of the United States, "burn" in Scotland and northeast England, and "beck" in northern England. Sometimes a river is defined as being larger than a creek, but not always: the language is vague."

http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_00001.value: A stream which, through permanent or seasonal flow processes, moves from elevated land towards lower elevations through a definite channel and empties either into a sea, lake, or another river or ends on land as bed seepage and evapotranspiration exceed water supply.

http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#com.value: "There are no official definitions for the generic term river as applied to geographic features, although in some countries or communities a stream is defined by its size. Many names for small rivers are specific to geographic location; examples are "run" in some parts of the United States, "burn" in Scotland and northeast England, and "beck" in northern England. Sometimes a river is defined as being larger than a creek, but not always: the language is vague."

definition.value: A surface landform which provides an egress for groundwater or steam to flow out of the ground.

definition.value: A valley or ravine, bounded by relatively steep banks, which in the rainy season becomes a watercourse.

definition.value: An area of water bordered by land on three sides.

definition.value: A body of water, usually of smaller size than a lake.

definition.value: An area of broken, fast flowing water in a stream, where the slope of the bed increases (but without a prominent break of slope which might result in a waterfall), or where a gently dipping bar of harder rock outcrops.

definition.value: A sheet of saline water separated from the open sea by sand or shingle banks. The sheet of water between an offshore reef, especially of coral and mainland. The sheet of water within a ring or horseshoe shaped atoll.
